May 2, 2026 · Origin: Ha Giang, Vietnam — 1,200–1,800m ASL

The Cloud-Forest Leaves: Vietnam's Ancient Shan Tuyet Tea

In a mist-soaked realm where limestone pinnacles pierce clouds at 1,800 meters and tea trees older than empires twist through primordial forest, the H'Mông and Dao guardians harvest leaves of impossible complexity — Shan Tuyet, the snow-covered mountain tea.

Shan Tuyet ancient tea is one of Vietnam's most distinctive agricultural products. Grown wild on 200+ year-old tea trees in the misty highlands of Ha Giang province, these hand-picked leaves yield a tea of remarkable smoothness and depth — a product that Greenie Vietnam now brings to global premium tea retailers and private label brands.

The Old Growth

These are not plantation rows of waist-high shrubs. Shan Tuyet trees stand 4 to 12 meters tall — true forest sentinels with trunks thick as ancient oaks, their canopies interweaving with wild orchids, epiphytic ferns, and the velvet moss unique to Ha Giang's limestone karst.

Dendrological Heritage: Core samples from the oldest specimens in Hoang Su Phi district confirm a minimum age of 270 years. These trees were already mature when the Tay Son Rebellion reshaped Vietnam in the 18th century.

The H'Mông and Dao ethnic communities who tend these trees do not farm them in any conventional sense. They are guardians of a wild ecosystem, harvesting only the "one bud, two leaves" standard — a protocol that respects the tree's physiological limits and ensures continued vitality.

At 1,200 to 1,800 meters above sea level, the trees endure winters where temperatures approach freezing on the highest ridges, only to be enveloped by thick montane clouds for up to 240 days per year. This perpetual altitude stress slows leaf metabolism dramatically, concentrating amino acids, catechins, and volatile aromatic compounds to levels rarely seen in cultivated teas.

In every cup of Shan Tuyet, you taste 270 years of rain, limestone, and cloud.

— Greenie Vietnam Tea Collection

The Sensory Architecture

Shan Tuyet defies the bitterness often associated with large-leaf teas.

What makes Shan Tuyet extraordinary is its absence of astringency. While most large-leaf teas carry a pronounced bitterness, Shan Tuyet's high-altitude amino acid concentration — particularly L-theanine — creates a natural sweetness that coats the palate. This is the direct result of stress metabolism: at 1,200 to 1,800 meters, cold nights and mist-shrouded days force the trees to produce protective compounds that translate into extraordinary cup complexity.

The leaves themselves tell the story. Each is thick and leathery — an adaptation to altitude — with a silvery down on the underside that gives the tea its name: "Shan Tuyet" means "snow on the mountain," referring both to the frost-touched peaks where the trees grow and the white-haired appearance of the young buds.

Tasting Notes

Dry Leaf Aroma: Wood-fired honey, aged cedar, and the faintest trace of wild jasmine from epiphytic companions in the forest canopy.

Liquor Color: Pale champagne gold with remarkable clarity — a visual signature of the high-altitude amino acid concentration.

First Infusion: A silken entry of chestnut honey and steamed rice cake, followed by a mid-palate that opens like wild orchid nectar — sweet but never cloying.

Finish: The "shan yun" (mountain rhyme) — a lingering mineral coolness reminiscent of limestone spring water, persisting for 15 minutes after the cup is empty.

The Harvest Calendar

The finest Shan Tuyet is harvested in three distinct windows:

  • Spring Harvest (March-April) — The crown jewel. Post-winter dormancy concentrates maximum sweetness and floral complexity. Our premium export grade.
  • Summer Harvest (June-July) — Bolder, more vegetal. Preferred by buyers seeking robust character for blending or daily consumption lines.
  • Autumn Harvest (September-October) — Mellow and smooth, with pronounced honey notes. Limited quantity, often reserved by advance contract.

The Ancient Tea Standard

Greenie selects only the "1 bud 2 leaves" standard from trees confirmed at 200+ years of age. Each batch undergoes moisture analysis (target: ≤5%), visual inspection for oxidation consistency, and cupping by a certified tea taster before approval.

Traditional withering on bamboo trays, hand-rolling in small batches, and oxidation monitored by artisans who learned the craft from their grandparents. No machine rolling, no artificial drying, no flavor additives.

  • Tree Age: 200+ years
  • Altitude: 1,200–1,800m
  • Cloud Cover: 240 days/year
  • Grade: 1 bud 2 leaves

Packaging Options

  • Loose leaf in kraft pouches — Ideal for specialty tea retailers and eco-conscious brands.
  • Premium tins — Gift-ready presentation for high-end retail and corporate markets.
  • Vacuum-sealed bulk packs — For tea houses, repackers, and blending operations.
  • Private label — Your brand, packaging, and label language. Full documentation support included.

Documents Available

  • Product specification sheets with tasting notes
  • Certificate of Analysis (COA)
  • SGS or Eurofins third-party testing on request
  • Country of Origin: Ha Giang, Vietnam
  • Altitude and harvest season statements
  • Shelf life and optimal storage guidance

Commercial Terms

  • MOQ: From 1 metric ton
  • Lead time: 7–15 days (standard packaging)
  • Incoterms: FOB or CIF from Saigon Port
  • Payment: T/T and L/C based on buyer requirements

Bring the Cloud Forest to Your Customers

Shan Tuyet ancient tea is a story in every cup — a product of old-growth trees, high-altitude mist, and generational craftsmanship. Greenie Vietnam invites premium tea retailers, specialty importers, and private label brands to evaluate this remarkable tea.
